changes.citation: Files in scope: backend/core/llm/prompts/parsing_prompt_pdf.txt, backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py, backend/app/routes/upload.py, frontend/app/types/parsed.ts, frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts, frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx, frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx, frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx. NOT in scope: backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py.
No .codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md mentioned in scope. No stash-as-convenience use identified.

Prompt lines 196-258: bounded to parse prompt, parser model, upload route passthrough, TS types, rangeAttentionLevel, ParsedTable badge, EditDialog contextual UI, page transformer. Non-goals (lines 249-258): no scoring policy, no fallback ranges, no second authority, no value-level inequality parsing, no broad parser redesign, no results/narrative work.

Prompt §Regression Targets (lines 305-333): PDF parse extraction (raw text preserved, context options extracted when present, one-sided inside options valid), review/edit behaviour (context workflow, selection populates min/max/unit, raw text visible, manual override, distinct attention state), analysis handoff (existing referenceRange contract unchanged, no scoring changes), safety/determinism (no fallback, no second authority, same choice = same payload, no silent inference).

STOP1: no scoring change required. STOP2: prompt-level extraction bounded by prompt Arch Decision 6 (omission/null on low confidence). STOP3: contextOptions are assistive-only (Arch Decision 1). STOP4: single active referenceRange contract preserved (referenceRangeToPayload unchanged). STOP5: user confirmation required when multiple options (Arch Decision 2) — no silent inference. STOP6: value-level inequality explicitly out of scope (prompt lines 186-188, STOP6). STOP7: EditDialog/ParsedTable changes are bounded additions, not broad flow redesign.

Risk 1: Gemini non-determinism for contextual extraction is pre-existing and accepted; mitigated by omission/null model (prompt §A, Arch Decision 6). Risk 2: rangeAttentionLevel new 'context-selection-required' branch — must fire when contextOptions.length > 1 AND no selectedContextId; must not conflict with 'one-sided' or 'partial'. Risk 3: buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow contextOptions passthrough must be additive and must not alter existing ref_low/ref_high logic for simple markers. Risk 4: All three caller sites (page.tsx, ParsedTable.tsx, uploadReferenceRange.ts) must be updated atomically to avoid TypeScript type mismatches.
No root cause compilers, signal evaluators, InsightGraph construction, or output assembly mechanisms in scope. STANDARD risk level is correct. No risk upgrade required.

All 8 gaps verified by reading actual source content: context_options absent from prompt schema (parsing_prompt_pdf.txt:4-18), ParsedBiomarker model (llm_parser.py:22-37), upload route passthrough (upload.py:146-148), TS interface (parsed.ts:6-27), RangeAttention type (uploadReferenceRange.ts:7), rangeAttentionLevel() (uploadReferenceRange.ts:89-102), rangeBadge() (ParsedTable.tsx:33-72), EditDialog contextual UI (EditDialog.tsx:79-433).
